justification: Boundaries correct: 10 is heavy (< 10 guard), 50 is freight (< 50 guard). All 5 ACs covered with explicit AC-to-test mapping in acceptance_criteria_coverage. No external dependencies. No features beyond spec. Confidence level 99 >= 85. Additional boundary cases (9.99, 49.99, 1000, negative) strengthen coverage without adding unrequested features.

All 5 acceptance criteria explicitly mapped to named tests in acceptance_criteria_coverage
Additional boundary tests (9.99, 49.99, 1000, negative) go beyond minimum without adding unrequested features
Pure function with no external dependencies
Assumptions are reasonable and clearly stated (upper bound inference, fractional weights)
No deviations or escalations — clean execution on a clear spec

observations.text: Boundary logic exactly matches spec: weight < 10 for standard, weight < 50 for heavy, so 10 → heavy and 50 → freight
All 5 ACs explicitly mapped to named tests with clear notes explaining the logic
Additional boundary tests (9.99, 49.99, 1000, negative) go beyond minimum without adding unspecified features
WeightValidationError as a typed subclass is a clean design choice within spec bounds
Assumptions are clearly stated and reasonable (1000 valid, negatives invalid)
Created package.json and tsconfig.json as extra scaffolding files — minor but not strictly required by the spec
